rule.name: Never skip reproduction
rule.description: If you can't reproduce the bug, you can't verify the fix works. Always ensure the bug is reproducible before proceeding with investigation.
rule.enforcement: Bob will require documented reproduction steps before moving to root cause analysis.

rule.name: Always add regression tests
rule.description: Every bug fix must include an automated test that would have caught the bug. This prevents the bug from returning in future changes.
rule.enforcement: Step 4 (Testing) is mandatory and must include a new regression test.

rule.name: Keep fixes focused
rule.description: Don't mix bug fixes with refactoring, new features, or unrelated changes. Each fix should address one specific bug.
rule.rationale: Focused fixes are easier to review, test, and rollback if needed.

rule.name: Document thoroughly
rule.description: Future developers need to understand why the bug occurred and how it was fixed. Include comments in code and detailed PR descriptions.

rule.name: Test comprehensively
rule.description: Verify the fix works AND ensure no new issues were introduced. Run full test suite, not just the new regression test.

rule.name: Update todo list after each step
rule.description: Track progress through the workflow using the update_todo_list tool.

rule.name: Get approval before deploying to production
rule.description: Ensure code review is complete and stakeholders are informed before production deployment.

step_rules.name: Reproduce the Bug
step_rules.rule: Document exact steps to reproduce
Capture all error messages and stack traces
Note the environment (OS, browser, version, etc.)
Verify bug exists in current version
Test if bug is consistent or intermittent

step_rules.name: Root Cause Analysis
step_rules.rule: Use debugging tools systematically
Check git history for related changes
Look for similar issues in issue tracker
Document the root cause clearly
Assess impact on other parts of the system

step_rules.name: Develop Fix
step_rules.rule: Fix the root cause, not just symptoms
Keep changes minimal and focused
Add comments explaining why the fix works
Consider backward compatibility
Handle edge cases properly

step_rules.name: Testing
step_rules.rule: Verify fix with original reproduction steps
Add regression test that would have caught the bug
Run full existing test suite
Test edge cases and error scenarios
MANDATORY: All tests must pass before proceeding

step_rules.name: Regression Prevention
step_rules.rule: Add automated test to test suite
Update documentation if bug revealed gaps
Search codebase for similar patterns
Consider if refactoring would prevent similar bugs
Document lessons learned

step_rules.name: Code Review and Documentation
step_rules.rule: Write clear PR description with context
Link PR to bug ticket
Include before/after behavior
Explain root cause in PR description
Respond to reviewer feedback promptly

step_rules.name: Deployment and Verification
step_rules.rule: Deploy to staging first
Verify fix in staging environment
Monitor logs after production deployment
Verify with original bug reporter
Update bug ticket status

standard.requirement: Fix addresses root cause, not symptoms
Regression test added to prevent recurrence

guideline.action: Immediately notify stakeholders and provide ETA for fix
Document the issue and discuss with team for future refactoring

guideline.situation: Workaround available while fix is in progress
guideline.action: Document workaround and communicate to affected users
Verify with original reporter and update knowledge base

practice.name: Use systematic debugging
practice.description: Follow a methodical approach: reproduce, isolate, identify, fix, verify. Don't jump to conclusions without evidence.

practice.name: Prefer simple fixes
practice.description: The simplest fix that addresses the root cause is usually the best. Avoid over-engineering the solution.

practice.name: Test like a user
practice.description: In addition to automated tests, manually test the fix as a user would. This often reveals issues automated tests miss.

practice.name: Learn from bugs
practice.description: Every bug is an opportunity to improve. Document what you learned and how similar bugs can be prevented.
